target.name: NVision Imaging Technologies
target.founder: Martin Plenio, Ilai Schwartz, Fedor Jelezko, Alex Retzker, Sella Brosh

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
NVision Imaging Technologies is a Series A biotech company founded by Martin Plenio, Ilai Schwartz, Fedor Jelezko, Alex Retzker, and Sella Brosh, having raised $20M to date. The valuation universe includes 3 public peers in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-sector comparables, though peer companies like Colossal Biosciences and Recursion Pharmaceuticals operate at different stages and scales.

Stage-progression analysis represents the only reliable triangulation method available. Using total capital raised ($20M) as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement yields a $40M valuation. The peer-median method is inapplicable; while 8 private biotech peers exist, fewer than three match narrow filters (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.9x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied without NVision's disclosed revenue.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $34M to $46M, with a median of $40M. This reflects stage-appropriate progression risk but carries material uncertainty. The absence of tight private-peer comparables and lack of revenue disclosure limit conviction. Key sensitivities include actual commercialization progress, IP strength, and team execution against frontier-tech development timelines—none of which are quantified in available data.
